name: Sterling Allen Brown
abstract: Sterling Allen Brown (May 1, 1901 – January 13, 1989) was an American professor, folklorist, poet, and literary critic. He chiefly studied black culture of the southern United States and was a professor at Howard University for most of his career.

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: he told me this years ago — Howard, like several other all-black colleges, would not allow jazz because it came from bad origins. So Sterling Brown would bring in a Milhaud piece...
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: He told me once that he was never allowed to teach jazz or blues as such...
